english_query: how paying close attention to what someone needs is a kind of good
ugf_response: . When you look at what another person really wants or has and try to give it, you are doing something that makes the world feel better for that one and for the other. This is not just a nice thing, but a way of being good. First, you see that every person has things they need that they do not have. Those things can be food, water, help from a place, a word that is heard, or any other small act that meets them. Second, you think about how to bring these needs into your own life. You may find that if you know what they want, you can ask simple questions like: does this make their body work? does this let them have enough money? Will this keep them safe? Third, you think if you can put yourself in the place of the other person and ask, Does this fit with what i already know? If the answer is no, you go back and look again at the words of the other, the places, and ask. By doing this you show that you care about the other person as much as you care about your own. Next, you do the same for more and more people. Each time you pay attention to the needs of another, you add a little more of the good feeling inside you, and the whole group feels stronger. a calm mind and a happy heart are ways to join together the pain of others with the happy feeling of yourself, and with the good of the many. So, looking at what a person needs and then giving it to them is a sort of good because it brings less hurt, more a sense of good, a quiet mind and a warm feel for both the one who gives and the one who gets, and the one where the one who receives is used in a way that helps and fits their need.
